meta.title: Lab — Antonio Lisboa
meta.description: Hands-on demos: React 19, Server Actions, Suspense, and next-intl working together.

Lab.title: Lab
Lab.intro: This page exists to show what the portfolio actually uses: client islands, server actions, Suspense streaming, and localized routing—without slide decks.

streaming.title: Streaming with Suspense
streaming.body: The block below comes from an async Server Component with an intentional delay—the fallback shows while the segment resolves.
streaming.ready: Async content ready—this simulates slow data or API calls without blocking the rest of the page.

action.title: Server Action + useActionState
action.body: The form calls a function marked with "use server" inside the same Next.js monolith. Returned state re-renders this card without an explicit REST API.
action.label: Your name (optional)

path.title: Typed routes (next-intl)
path.body: The pathname below comes from createNavigation—handy for active states and analytics without magic strings.
path.label: usePathname() in this locale
path.hint: Updates when you switch EN / ES / DE—the prefix follows localePrefix: as-needed.

terminal.body: Client-only animation—useful for micro-interactions and dev onboarding.

eggs.title: Easter eggs
eggs.body: There are at least three surprises: a classic key chord, clicks on the hero kicker, and a sequence in the footer. Explore freely.

meta.title: Work — Antonio Lisboa
meta.description: Real cases: healthtech, edtech, and a public-sector build with integrations and AI.

Work.title: Work in depth
Work.intro: Each block summarizes context, my role, and technical impact. Private code is only described at a high level.
Work.labLink: See how this site is built (Lab)

items.title: Vittahub — healthcare marketplace
items.context: Early-stage startup building a marketplace for clinics and continuous care. Focus on discovery, requirements, and delivery cadence with a small team and QA.
impact: System design aligned with business requirements and an incremental roadmap.
Culture of frequent releases with DevOps and data/model reviews.
Technical mentoring and pair programming with interns.
items.note: Private repository and data—no public demo.

items.title: Ussin — educational platform
items.context: Educational product with complex graph flows and strong inclusion and multilingual requirements.
impact: Replaced the graph engine with React Flow for clear performance wins.
Automatic language from user locale and accessibility improvements aligned to international references.
items.stack: React, React Flow, Node.js, MariaDB, i18n

items.title: Federal University of Ceará — institutional module
items.context: Freelance project for the university: institutional module in the official app with bookings, schedules, and student support with AI and automation.
impact: Room reservations and automatic per-room schedules.
AI for FAQs connected to the app and WhatsApp via N8N.
Full stack deployed with Docker on the university's own servers.
items.coverAlt: Abstract 3D illustration — Federal University institutional module

hero.headlineAria: I lead technical teams, plan sprints, plan roadmaps, shape system architecture, ship fullstack products, build mobile and web, mentor interns, structure backlogs, facilitate agile ceremonies, and align discovery with delivery.
hero.title: Antonio Lisboa
hero.bioLine1: I'm a fullstack developer, currently serving as Tech Lead and Scrum Master at Vittahub, a healthcare marketplace in Brazil — React, Next.js, TypeScript, Node.js, and PostgreSQL with recurring delivery.
hero.bioLine1Mobile: I'm a fullstack developer, Tech Lead and Scrum Master at Vittahub — a healthcare marketplace in Brazil.
hero.bioLine2: I hold a B.Sc. in Computer Science from Federal University of Ceará (UFC), with a focus on usability, accessibility, and performance. Browse my projects or GitHub.
hero.summary: Fullstack engineer (React/Next.js, TypeScript, Node.js, SQL) with technical leadership at Vittahub and delivery across healthcare, public-sector, and edtech. Emphasis on usability, accessibility, performance, and quality.

homeWorkTeaser.title: Case studies
homeWorkTeaser.body: Narrative, impact, and stack per project.

stages.title: Overview
stages.text: I'm a co-founder of Vittahub, a healthcare marketplace in Brazil. What started as a lean startup has grown into a team of more than 12 collaborators — including 8 developers. Today I combine product design, Scrum Master, Tech Lead, and software architecture design: shaping the product vision, structuring the backlog, facilitating ceremonies, guiding technical decisions, and staying hands-on in fullstack delivery with the team.

stages.title: From startup to team
stages.text: What started as a lean startup has grown into a team of more than 12 collaborators — including 8 developers. I helped shape hiring, onboarding, and delivery cadence as the product scaled.

stages.title: Meetings
stages.text: We run weekly ceremonies as a team: sprint planning, daily syncs when needed, sprint review with stakeholders, and retrospectives to adjust process and delivery cadence.

stages.title: Product & tech leadership
stages.text: I combine product design, Scrum Master, Tech Lead, and software architecture: shaping the product vision, structuring the backlog, facilitating ceremonies, guiding technical decisions, and staying hands-on in fullstack delivery.

stages.title: Overview
stages.text: As founder of Alémsys Digital I run a software studio delivering custom web and mobile products for regional businesses — from discovery through build, maintenance, and iteration — including the studio platform at alemys.digital.

stages.title: Custom products
stages.text: End-to-end delivery of tailored web and mobile products for regional businesses, with direct client discovery and iterative releases.

stages.title: Studio platform
stages.text: Built and launched the studio platform at alemys.digital — portfolio, lead capture, and the operational backbone for client work.

stages.title: Mobile version
stages.text: Custom mobile apps with the same delivery cadence: direct client discovery, iterative releases, and a focus on performance and usability on small screens.

stages.title: Desktop version
stages.text: Web products and management dashboards built for wide screens: architecture, complex flows, and iterative delivery with a focus on clarity and performance on desktop.

stages.title: App in production
stages.text: Shipping and evolving mobile apps in production: metrics, user feedback, fixes, and new releases without losing delivery cadence with the client.

stages.title: Support & evolution
stages.text: Ongoing maintenance and evolution of shipped products: client support, incremental improvements, and roadmap alignment with the business.

stages.title: Overview
stages.text: Freelance delivery of an institutional module for the university app: room booking with automated per-room schedules, AI-powered student FAQs wired to the app and WhatsApp via N8N, all hosted on the university's own servers with Docker.

stages.title: Official app
stages.text: Module integrated into the university's institutional app — the foundation for room booking, AI-powered support, and automation on the campus's own infrastructure.

stages.title: Digital ecosystem
stages.text: The module in campus context: connected services and flows across the app, WhatsApp, and the university's on-premise infrastructure.

items.title: Fullstack Web Developer
items.stack: React, React Flow, Node.js, MariaDB, i18n

stages.title: Overview
stages.text: Fullstack role on an educational platform with complex graph flows, multilingual requirements, and a web-to-mobile migration — replacing the graph engine with React Flow and improving accessibility aligned to international standards.

stages.title: React Flow migration
stages.text: Migrated the graph engine to React Flow — roughly 4× faster rendering on the heaviest learning paths.

stages.title: Web to mobile
stages.text: Web-to-mobile migration with redesigned flows for smaller screens — same learning logic, adapted for touch.

stages.title: i18n & accessibility
stages.text: Automatic language from user locale plus accessibility improvements aligned to international references across the learning experience.
